report_name: Global Takedown Disrupts 'Tycoon 2FA' Phishing Service That Bypassed MFA for 100k Orgs

threat_actors.name: Tycoon 2FA
threat_actors.description: A criminal enterprise that operated a Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform, selling a toolkit to low-skilled cybercriminals to conduct adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) attacks to bypass MFA and steal credentials and session cookies. The platform was active since August 2023 and targeted nearly 100,000 organizations.
threat_actors.goals: To provide phishing tools as a service for financial gain, enabling other criminals to steal credentials and session cookies from a wide range of organizations.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The Tycoon 2FA platform generated phishing links that were sent to targets to initiate the adversary-in-the-middle attack.

attack_patterns.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
attack_patterns.description: The Tycoon 2FA platform used adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) techniques, employing a reverse-proxy server to intercept communications between the victim and a legitimate service like Microsoft 365.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Interception
attack_patterns.description: The AitM reverse proxy intercepted the MFA one-time passcode entered by the victim and relayed it to the legitimate service, allowing the attacker to bypass MFA protections like SMS, email, and TOTP.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
attack_patterns.description: The core of the Tycoon 2FA attack was to intercept the session cookie issued by the legitimate service after a successful MFA login. This cookie was then provided to the criminal, allowing them to hijack the authenticated session.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The primary goal of the phishing campaigns enabled by Tycoon 2FA was to steal credentials and session tokens to gain access to valid user accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: Once an account was compromised using the stolen session cookie, attackers could engage in activities such as data theft, including email collection from the victim's mailbox.

tools.name: Tycoon 2FA Phishing Toolkit
tools.description: A Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) toolkit that provides a reverse-proxy infrastructure to perform adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) attacks, steal credentials and session cookies, and bypass multi-factor authentication.

tools.name: Cloudflare Workers
tools.description: The Tycoon 2FA platform abused the legitimate Cloudflare Workers service to host its malicious reverse-proxy logic, making the traffic difficult to block as it appeared to originate from Cloudflare's trusted IP space.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Led the international law enforcement operation to disrupt the Tycoon 2FA platform.

identities.name: Europol
identities.description: A key partner in the international law enforcement operation that dismantled the Tycoon 2FA infrastructure.

identities.name: Cloudflare
identities.description: Supported the takedown of Tycoon 2FA, whose platform abused Cloudflare Workers.

identities.name: Microsoft 365
identities.description: A primary target for impersonation by the Tycoon 2FA phishing platform, which mimicked its login pages to steal credentials.

identities.name: Schools
identities.description: A category of organizations targeted by criminals using the Tycoon 2FA phishing platform.

identities.name: Hospitals
identities.description: A category of critical sector organizations targeted by criminals using the Tycoon 2FA phishing platform.

infrastructure.name: Tycoon 2FA Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The core infrastructure of the Tycoon 2FA service, consisting of over 330 domains and reverse-proxy servers used to conduct adversary-in-the-middle phishing attacks. This infrastructure was seized in a global takedown.

indicators.name: Tycoon 2FA - Anomalous Geolocation Logon
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Geolocation Logon Pattern
id: 9b8c1a2d-3e4f-5a6b-7c8d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious login patterns, such as logins from unfamiliar locations, ISPs, or devices immediately following a successful MFA authentication from a known location. This can be an indicator of an Adversary-in-the-Middle (AitM) session hijacking attack.
references:
  - https://www.diesec.com/blog/top-5-cybersecurity-news-stories-march-13-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-03-13
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: 0
    ConditionalAccessStatus: 'success'
  filter:
    # Add known corporate ASN or IP ranges here
    IpAddress_as_number:
      - 12345 # Example corporate ASN
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user travel
  - Use of VPNs by legitimate users
indicators.description: Detects suspicious login patterns, such as logins from unfamiliar geolocations or ASNs, which could indicate a session cookie has been stolen and is being used by an attacker, as seen with Tycoon 2FA.

indicators.name: Tycoon 2FA - Suspicious Cloudflare Worker Proxy Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Cloudflare Worker Proxy Activity
id: 8c7b2d1a-4f5e-6b7c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious web traffic patterns involving Cloudflare Workers, which may be abused for reverse-proxying phishing sites, as seen with the Tycoon 2FA platform.
references:
  - https://www.diesec.com/blog/top-5-cybersecurity-news-stories-march-13-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-03-13
tags:
  - attack.t1557
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith: '.workers.dev'
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'login'
      - 'signin'
      - 'mfa'
      - 'auth'
      - 'session'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate applications that use Cloudflare Workers for authentication or session management.
indicators.description: Detects web proxy traffic to Cloudflare Workers domains containing keywords related to authentication, a technique used by Tycoon 2FA to hide its reverse-proxy infrastructure.

relationships.source_name: Tycoon 2FA - Suspicious Cloudflare Worker Proxy Activity
